author: gb9t
content:
**00-security-principles.md authored 2026-05-09. Issue resolved.**

.design/00-security-principles.md (348 lines, 10 REQ / 10 AC, 0 open).

Distillation of the security policy into a Tier-1 binding reference. Cites cleanly back to security/00-overview.md, references/grsec-pax-notes.md, 00-overview.md.

Four foundational axioms locked:
1. Memory-protection + CFI features absorb fully and default ON
2. Policy-enforcement features go through GR-RBAC, never default-on system-wide
3. LSM framework is intact; GR-RBAC stacks alongside SELinux/AppArmor
4. Userspace-visible behavior changes only through documented opt-out / opt-in mechanisms

Concrete spec content:

NEW ABI (Rookery-original) for JIT-process exemption:
- ELF note section: '.note.rookery.security'
- Note name: 'Rookery\\0' (8 bytes, null-terminated)
- Note type: NT_ROOKERY_SECURITY_FLAGS = 0x52455253
- Flag bits: bit 0 = ROOKERY_SECURITY_NEEDS_EXEC_GAIN; bit 1 = ROOKERY_SECURITY_NEEDS_RWX_ANON; bits 2-31 reserved
- prctl: PR_REQUEST_EXEC_GAIN = 80 (subject to upstream prctl assignment confirmation at impl time)
- Sticky once granted; clone(2) inherits; execve(2) resets per new ELF note
- PR_SET_NO_NEW_PRIVS prevents PR_REQUEST_EXEC_GAIN (-EPERM)
- prctl(PR_SET_MDWE) preserved unchanged; orthogonal — tightens on top of Rookery default

Knob inventory (locked, binding):
- 16 Kconfig symbols with defaults (CONFIG_CFI_CLANG=y, CONFIG_RANDSTRUCT_FULL=y, CONFIG_RANDOMIZE_KSTACK_OFFSET=y, CONFIG_INIT_ON_FREE_DEFAULT_ON=y, CONFIG_LATENT_ENTROPY=y, CONFIG_DELAY_FREE_PAGES=y [Rookery-new], CONFIG_LSM_GRBAC=y, ...)
- 17 sysctls with defaults (kernel.randomize_kstack_offset=1 flipped, kernel.dmesg_restrict=1 flipped, vm.zero_on_free=1 flipped, vm.delay_free_pages=1 Rookery-new, fs.protected_fifos=2 flipped, fs.protected_regular=2 flipped, ...)
- 6 kernel cmdline params with defaults (init_on_alloc=1 flipped, slab_nomerge=y, ...)
- 4 prctls (PR_SET_MDWE existing, PR_REQUEST_EXEC_GAIN=80 new, ...)
- 1 ELF note (NT_ROOKERY_SECURITY_FLAGS)
- LSM stack order: 'lockdown,yama,loadpin,safesetid,integrity,selinux,smack,tomoyo,apparmor,bpf,landlock,grbac' (GR-RBAC last)

Per-Tier-3 Hardening section template provided. Every Tier-3 doc must cite this document for any default it relies on; cannot graduate from draft without a Hardening section.

Userspace-visible behavior changes from upstream defaults enumerated (per Axiom 4):
- Stricter sysctls: dmesg_restrict, fs.protected_fifos, fs.protected_regular, kptr_restrict=2 (vs upstream 0)
- New behaviors with opt-out: zero_on_free, randomize_kstack_offset, delay_free_pages, init_on_alloc
- New ABI for opt-in: exec-gain ELF note + prctl
- All others match upstream

Cross-refs:
- 00-overview.md (keystone)
- 00-rust-conventions.md (type-system enforcement of mandatory features)
- references/grsec-pax-notes.md (catalog with two-layer split)
- security/00-overview.md (per-feature implementation-home table)
- security/grbac/00-overview.md (GR-RBAC LSM Tier-3, issue #5 — to be authored)
- mm/00-overview.md § mmap.md, fs/00-overview.md § exec-binfmt.md, kernel/00-overview.md § task-lifecycle.md, arch/x86/00-overview.md § cpu-mitigations.md (implementation homes)
- 00-glossary.md

Knowledge page: 00-security-principles (tags: design-doc, foundation, security)

This issue is now resolved. The doc itself has 0 open questions; future amendments use --kind decision comments here per REQ-6/REQ-10.

Phase B + this Tier-1 are complete. Phase C (component-level Tier-3 designs) is now unblocked.
